 31→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.lastChildLeafOrSelf
 32→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextCodeLeaf
 33→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
 34→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevCodeLeaf
 35→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 36→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe
 37→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe
 38→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.util.safeAs
 39→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 40→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 41→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 42→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiWhiteSpaceImpl
 43→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.descriptors.annotations.AnnotationUseSiteTarget
 44→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtAnnotationEntry
 45→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.leaves
 46→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.siblings
 47→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.utils.addToStdlib.applyIf
 48→
 49→/**
 50→ * Ensures that annotation are wrapped to separate lines.
 51→ *
 52→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#annotation-formatting
 53→ *
 54→ * 1) Place annotations on separate lines before the declaration to which they are attached, and with the same indentation:
 55→ *
 56→ *    ```
 57→ *    @Target(AnnotationTarget.PROPERTY)
 58→ *    annotation class JsonExclude
 59→ *    ```
 60→ *
 61→ * 2) Annotations without arguments may be placed on the same line:
 62→ *
 63→ *    ```
 64→ *    @JsonExclude @JvmField
 65→ *    var x: String
 66→ *    ```
 67→ *
 68→ * 3) A single annotation without arguments may be placed on the same line as the corresponding declaration:
 69→ *
 70→ *    ```
 71→ *    @Test fun foo() { /*...*/ }
 72→ *    ```
 73→ *
 74→ * 4) File annotations are placed after the file comment (if any), before the package statement, and are separated from package with a blank
 75→ *    line (to emphasize the fact that they target the file and not the package).
 76→ *
 77→ * @see [AnnotationSpacingRule] for white space rules.
 78→ */
 79→public class AnnotationRule :
 80→    StandardRule(
 81→        id = "annotation",
 82→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 83→            setOf(
 84→                CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 85→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 86→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 87→            ),
 88→        visitorModifiers =
 89→            setOf(
 90→                RunAfterRule(
 91→                    ruleId = ENUM_WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 92→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 93→                ),
 94→            ),
 95→    ) {
 96→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 97→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 98→
 99→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
100→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
101→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
102→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
103→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
104→        )
105→    }
106→
107→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
108→        node: ASTNode,
109→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
110→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
111→    ) {
112→        when (node.elementType) {
113→            FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST -> {
114→                visitFileAnnotationList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
115→            }
116→            ANNOTATION -> {
117→                // Annotation array
118→                //     @[...]
119→                visitAnnotation(node, emit, autoCorrect)
120→            }
121→            ANNOTATION_ENTRY -> {
122→                visitAnnotationEntry(node, emit, autoCorrect)
123→            }
124→        }
125→    }
126→
127→    private fun visitAnnotationEntry(
128→        node: ASTNode,
129→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
130→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
131→    ) {
132→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
133→
134→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.isPrimaryConstructorAnnotation()) {
135→            visitPrimaryConstructorAnnotation(node, emit, autoCorrect)
136→            return
137→        }
138→
139→        if (node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() &&
140→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
141→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
142→            node.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
143→        ) {
144→            // Disallow:
145→            //     @Foo class Bar
146→            // Allow function parameters and arguments to have annotation on same line as identifier:
147→            //     fun @receiver:Bar String.foo() {}
148→            //     fun foo(
149→            //         @Bar("bar") bar,
150→            //         @Bar("bar") "42",
151→            //     ) {}
152→            checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
153→        }
154→
155→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()) {
156→            if (node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
157→                // Code below is disallowed
158→                //   @Foo1
159→                //   @Foo2 fun foo() {}
160→                emit(
161→                    node.startOffset,
162→                    "Annotation must be placed on a separate line when it is preceded by another annotation on a separate line",
163→                    true,
164→                )
165→                if (autoCorrect) {
166→                    node
167→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
168→                        .nextLeaf()
169→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
170→                }
171→            }
172→
173→            if (node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
174→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
175→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
176→                node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
177→                node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
178→            ) {
179→                // Code below is disallowed
180→                //   @Foo1 @Foo2 fun foo() {}
181→                // But following is allowed:
182→                //   @[Foo1 Foo2] fun foo() {}
183→                //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
184→                emit(
185→                    node.findAnnotatedConstruct().startOffset,
186→                    "Multiple annotations should not be placed on the same line as the annotated construct",
187→                    true,
188→                )
189→                if (autoCorrect) {
190→                    node
191→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
192→                        .nextCodeLeaf()
193→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
194→                            getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)
195→                                .applyIf(node.typeProjectionOrNull() != null) {
196→                                    plus(indentConfig.indent)
197→                                },
198→                        )
199→                }
200→            }
201→
202→            node.typeProjectionOrNull()
203→                ?.prevCodeLeaf()
204→                ?.let { startOfList ->
205→                    // Code below is disallowed
206→                    //   var foo: List<@Foo1 @Foo2 String>
207→                    // But following is allowed:
208→                    //   var foo: List<@[Foo1 Foo2] String>
209→                    //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
210→                    if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
211→                        node.isFirstAnnotationEntry()
212→                    ) {
213→                        emit(startOfList.startOffset, "Expected newline after '${startOfList.text}'", true)
214→                        if (autoCorrect) {
215→                            startOfList
216→                                .upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(
217→                                    getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).plus(indentConfig.indent),
218→                                )
219→                        }
220→                    }
221→                    if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
222→                        node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
223→                    ) {
224→                        node
225→                            .findAnnotatedConstruct()
226→                            .treeParent
227→                            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
228→                            .nextLeaf { it.isCodeLeaf() && it.elementType != ElementType.COMMA }
229→                            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
230→                                emit(codeLeaf.startOffset, "Expected newline before '${codeLeaf.text}'", true)
231→                                if (autoCorrect) {
232→                                    codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
233→                                }
234→                            }
235→                    }
236→                }
237→        }
238→
239→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() && node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
240→            // Code below is disallowed
241→            //   @Foo1
242→            //   @Foo2 @Foo3
243→            //   fun foo() {}
244→            emit(
245→                node.startOffset,
246→                "All annotations should either be on a single line or all annotations should be on a separate line",
247→                true,
248→            )
249→            if (autoCorrect) {
250→                node
251→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
252→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
253→            }
254→        }
255→    }
256→
257→    private fun ASTNode.typeProjectionOrNull() =
258→        takeIf { elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
259→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST }
260→            ?.treeParent
261→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE }
262→            ?.treeParent
263→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION }
264→            ?.treeParent
265→
266→    private fun ASTNode.isPrimaryConstructorAnnotation() =
267→        takeIf { elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
268→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST }
269→            ?.treeParent
270→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR }
271→            .let { it != null }
272→
273→    private fun visitPrimaryConstructorAnnotation(
274→        node: ASTNode,
275→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
276→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
277→    ) {
278→        val expectedIndent = getNewlineWithIndentForPrimaryConstructorAnnotation(node)
279→        if (node.isFirstAnnotationEntry()) {
280→            // Annotation before constructor should be on separate line from class identifier:
281→            //    data class Foo @Bar1 constructor()
282→            // should be formatted as:
283→            //    data class Foo
284→            //        @Bar1
285→            //        constructor()
286→            node
287→                .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
288→                .prevLeaf()
289→                ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE && !it.textContains('\n') }
290→                ?.let {
291→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Expected newline before annotation", true)
292→                    if (autoCorrect) {
293→                        node.firstChildLeafOrSelf().upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expectedIndent)
294→                    }
295→                }
296→        }
297→        if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine()) {
298→            // Each annotation should be on a separate line:
299→            //    data class Foo @Bar1 @Bar2 constructor()
300→            // should be formatted as:
301→            //    data class Foo
302→            //        @Bar1
303→            //        @Bar2
304→            //        constructor()
305→            emit(node.startOffset, "Expected each annotation to be on a separate line", true)
306→            if (autoCorrect) {
307→                node.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextLeaf()?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expectedIndent)
308→            }
309→        }
310→        if (node.isLastAnnotationEntry()) {
311→            // Last annotation should be on separate line from constructor keyword (or visibility modifier):
312→            //    data class Foo @Bar1 constructor()
313→            //    data class Foo @Bar1 internal constructor()
314→            // should be formatted as:
315→            //    data class Foo
316→            //        @Bar1
317→            //        constructor()
318→            //    data class Foo
319→            //        @Bar1
320→            //        internal constructor()
321→            node
322→                .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
323→                .nextLeaf()
324→                ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE && !it.textContains('\n') }
325→                ?.let { whiteSpaceAfterAnnotation ->
326→                    val nextCodeLeaf = whiteSpaceAfterAnnotation.nextCodeLeaf()
327→                    if (nextCodeLeaf != null) {
328→                        emit(nextCodeLeaf.startOffset, "Expected newline", true)
329→                        if (autoCorrect) {
330→                            nextCodeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expectedIndent)
331→                        }
332→                    }
333→                }
334→        }
335→    }
336→
337→    private fun getNewlineWithIndentForPrimaryConstructorAnnotation(node: ASTNode): String {
338→        // For primary constructor annotations, the indent should be computed from the CLASS element
339→        // node.treeParent = MODIFIER_LIST
340→        // node.treeParent.treeParent = PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR
341→        // node.treeParent.treeParent.treeParent = CLASS
342→        val classNode =
343→            node
344→                .treeParent
345→                .treeParent
346→                .treeParent
347→        val nodeBeforeClass = classNode.treePrev
348→        // Make sure we only insert a single newline
349→        val classIndent =
350→            nodeBeforeClass
351→                ?.text
352→                .orEmpty()
353→                .substringAfterLast('\n')
354→        return "\n".plus(classIndent).plus(indentConfig.indent)
355→    }
356→
357→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine(): Boolean {
358→        val firstAnnotation = treeParent.findChildByType(ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
359→        return siblings(forward = false)
360→            .takeWhile { it != firstAnnotation }
361→            .any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
362→    }
363→
364→    private fun checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
365→        node: ASTNode,
366→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
367→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
368→    ) {
369→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry()) {
370→            emit(
371→                node.startOffset,
372→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
373→                true,
374→            )
375→            if (autoCorrect) {
376→                node
377→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
378→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
379→            }
380→        }
381→
382→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct()) {
383→            emit(
384→                node.startOffset,
385→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
386→                // Annotated expressions for which the annotation contains a parameter can be hard to correct
387→                // automatically. See examples below. For now, let them be fixed manually.
388→                // fun foo1() = @Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()
389→                // if (@Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()) { .. }
390→                node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION,
391→            )
392→            if (autoCorrect) {
393→                node
394→                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
395→                    .nextLeaf()
396→                    .safeAs<LeafPsiElement>()
397→                    ?.let {
398→                        if (it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
399→                            it.replaceWithText(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
400→                        } else {
401→                            it.rawInsertBeforeMe(
402→                                PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)),
403→                            )
404→                        }
405→                    }
406→            }
407→        }
408→    }
409→
410→    private fun ASTNode.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation() = getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() != AnnotationUseSiteTarget.RECEIVER
411→
412→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() =
413→        psi
414→            .safeAs<KtAnnotationEntry>()
415→            ?.useSiteTarget
416→            ?.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget()
417→
418→    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() = getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() != null
419→
420→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() =
421→        takeIf { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
422→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST)
423→
424→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstAnnotationEntry() =
425→        this ==
426→            treeParent
427→                .children()
428→                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
429→
430→    private fun ASTNode.isLastAnnotationEntry() =
431→        this ==
432→            treeParent
433→                .children()
434→                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
435→
436→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
437→        siblings(forward = false)
438→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
439→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
440→
441→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
442→        siblings()
443→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
444→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
445→
446→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() =
447→        siblings(forward = false)
448→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
449→
450→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry() =
451→        siblings(forward = false)
452→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
453→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
454→
455→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() = siblings(forward = true).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
456→
457→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry() =
458→        siblings(forward = true)
459→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
460→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
461→
462→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(): Boolean {
463→        val annotatedConstruct = findAnnotatedConstruct()
464→        return lastChildLeafOrSelf()
465→            .leaves(forward = true)
466→            .takeWhile { it != annotatedConstruct }
467→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
468→    }
469→
470→    private fun ASTNode.findAnnotatedConstruct(): ASTNode {
471→        val astNode =
472→            if (treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST) {
473→                treeParent
474→            } else {
475→                this
476→            }
477→        return checkNotNull(
478→            astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextCodeLeaf(),
479→        )
480→    }
481→
482→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct() =
483→        if (isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
484→            isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()
485→        } else {
486→            isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
487→        }
488→
489→    private fun visitFileAnnotationList(
490→        node: ASTNode,
491→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
492→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
493→    ) {
494→        node
495→            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
496→            .nextCodeLeaf()
497→            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
498→                val whitespaceBefore = codeLeaf.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
499→
500→                if (whitespaceBefore == null || whitespaceBefore.text != "\n\n") {
501→                    emit(
502→                        codeLeaf.startOffset,
503→                        "File annotations should be separated from file contents with a blank line",
504→                        true,
505→                    )
506→                    if (autoCorrect) {
507→                        codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n\n")
508→                    }
509→                }
510→            }
511→    }
512→
513→    private fun visitAnnotation(
514→        node: ASTNode,
515→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
516→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
517→    ) {
518→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION)
519→
520→        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
521→            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry())
522→        ) {
523→            emit(
524→                node.startOffset,
525→                "@[...] style annotations should be on a separate line from other annotations.",
526→                true,
527→            )
528→            if (autoCorrect) {
529→                if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
530→                    node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
531→                } else if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
532→                    node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
533→                }
534→            }
535→        }
536→    }
537→
538→    private fun getNewlineWithIndent(modifierListRoot: ASTNode): String {
539→        val nodeBeforeAnnotations =
540→            modifierListRoot
541→                .treeParent
542→                .treePrev
543→        // Make sure we only insert a single newline
544→        val indentWithoutNewline =
545→            nodeBeforeAnnotations
546→                ?.text
547→                .orEmpty()
548→                .substringAfterLast('\n')
549→        return "\n".plus(indentWithoutNewline)
550→    }
551→}
552→
553→public val ANNOTATION_RULE_ID: RuleId = AnnotationRule().ruleId
554→

conversation_history.name: read
conversation_history.content:
1→# Changelog
 2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
 3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
 4→
 5→## Unreleased
 6→
 7→WARNING: This version on KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgrade before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
 8→
 9→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to migrate to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
10→- The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI and `.editorconfig`.
11→- The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
12→- The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
13→
14→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
15→
16→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
17→
18→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
19→* The `.editorconfig` setting `disabled_rules`.
20→* KtLint disable and enable directives.
21→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
22→
23→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
24→
25→#### Experimental rules
26→
27→Rules in custom rule sets can be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint whenever the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled.
28→
29→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set. A rule is identified by its qualified rule id that includes the rule set id. Moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set has the downside that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
30→
31→#### EditorConfig
32→
33→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. Note that also new fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
34→
35→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
36→
37→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
38→
39→A new code style with name `ktlint_offical` has been added. This code style makes it possible to innovate ktlint beyond current expectations of users that are familiar with the existing code styles. 
40→
41→The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
42→
43→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
44→
45→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
46→
47→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
48→
49→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
50→
51→This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
52→
53→Classes below have been moved to the new module `ktlint-rule-engine`:
54→
55→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine-core`                               |
56→|----------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
57→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig               | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig                            |
58→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties     | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig                            |
59→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.OptInFeatures              | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.OptInFeatures                           |
60→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType                | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType                             |

220→
221→#### `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` default value
222→
223→Previously the default value for `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` was set to `-1` in ktlint unless the property was defined explicitly in the `.editorconfig` or when `ktlint_code_style` was set to Android. As a result of that rules have to check that max_line_length contains a positive value before checking that the actual line length is exceeding the maximum. Now the value `Int.MAX_VALUE` (use constant `MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF` to refer to that value) is used instead. 
224→
225→### Added
226→
227→* Add new experimental rule `no-empty-first-line-in-class-body` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rule disallows a class to start with a blank line. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
228→* Add new experimental rule `if-else-bracing` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rules enforces consistent usage of braces in all branches of a singe if or if-else-if statement. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
229→* Add new experimental rule `no-consecutive-comments` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rule disallows consecutive comments except EOL comments. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
230→* Add new experimental rule `try-catch-finally-spacing` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rule enforces consistent spacing in try-catch, try-finally and try-catch-finally statement. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
231→* Wrap the type or value of a function or class parameter in case the maximum line length is exceeded `parameter-wrapping` ([#1846](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1846))
232→* Wrap the type or value of a property in case the maximum line length is exceeded `property-wrapping` ([#1846](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1846))
233→* Recognize Kotlin Script when linting and formatting code from `stdin` with KtLint CLI ([#1832](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1832))
234→* Add new experimental rule `no-blank-line-in-list` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rule disallows blank lines to be used in super type lists, type argument lists, type constraint lists, type parameter lists, value argument lists, and value parameter lists. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled. ([#1224](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1224))
235→* Add new experimental rule `multiline-expression-wrapping` for `ktlint_official` code style. This forces a multiline expression as value in an assignment to start on a separate line. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled. ([#1217](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1217))
236→* Add new experimental rule `string-template-indent` for `ktlint_official` code style. This forces multiline string templates which are post-fixed with `.trimIndent()` to be formatted consistently. The opening and closing `"""` are placed on separate lines and the indentation of the content of the template is aligned with the `"""`. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled. ([#925](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/925))
237→* Support Bill of Materials (BOM), now you can integrate Ktlint in your `build.gradle` like:
238→  ```kotlin
239→  dependencies {
240→      implementation(platform("com.pinterest:ktlint-bom:0.49.0"))
241→      implementation("com.pinterest:ktlint-core")
242→      implementation("com.pinterest:ktlint-reporter-html")
243→      implementation("com.pinterest:ktlint-ruleset-standard")
244→      ...
245→  }
246→  ```
247→* Add new experimental rule `if-else-wrapping` for `ktlint_official` code style. This enforces that a single line if-statement is kept simple. A single line if-statement may contain no more than one else-branch. The branches a single line if-statement may not be wrapped in a block. ([#812](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/812))
248→* Add new experimental rule `enum-wrapping` for all code styles. An enum should either be a single line, or each enum entry should be defined on a separate line. ([#1903](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1903))
249→
250→### Removed
251→
252→* Remove support of the `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules`. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
253→
254→### Fixed
255→
256→* An enumeration class having a primary constructor and in which the list of enum entries is followed by a semicolon then do not remove the semicolon in case it is followed by code element `no-semi` ([#1733](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1733))
257→* Do not add the (first line of the) body expression on the same line as the function signature in case the max line length would be exceeded. `function-signature`. 
258→* Do not add the first line of a multiline body expression on the same line as the function signature in case function body expression wrapping property is set to `multiline`. `function-signature`. 
259→* Disable the `standard:filename` rule whenever Ktlint CLI is run with option `--stdin` ([#1742](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1742))

content.text:
Let me provide a summary of all the changes I made to resolve this issue:

## Summary of Changes

### 1. AnnotationRule.kt
- Added `CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY` to `usesEditorConfigProperties` set
- Added necessary imports (`CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official`, `PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR`)
- Added `isPrimaryConstructorAnnotation()` function to detect if an annotation is part of a primary constructor's modifier list
- Added `visitPrimaryConstructorAnnotation()` function to handle wrapping annotations before the primary constructor:
  - Checks if the first annotation needs a newline before it (separating from class name)
  - Ensures each annotation is on its own line
  - Ensures there's a newline after the last annotation (before visibility modifier or constructor keyword)
- Added `getNewlineWithIndentForPrimaryConstructorAnnotation()` to compute the correct indentation from the class-level context
- The feature is only enabled when `codeStyle == ktlint_official`

### 2. AnnotationRuleTest.kt
- Added imports for `CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY` and `ktlint_official`
- Added a nested test class with comprehensive tests covering:
  - Single annotation before primary constructor
  - Multiple annotations before primary constructor
  - Annotation with visibility modifier (e.g., `internal`)
  - Already correctly formatted annotations (no violations)
  - Class with generic type parameter
  - Nested class with annotation before primary constructor

### 3. CHANGELOG.md
- Added changelog entry documenting the new feature

## How It Works

When using `ktlint_official` code style, code like:
```kotlin
data class Foo @Bar1 @Bar2("bar") @Bar3 @Bar4 constructor(private val foobar: Int) {
    fun foo(): String = "foo"
}
```

Will be formatted as:
```kotlin
data class Foo
    @Bar1
    @Bar2("bar")
    @Bar3
    @Bar4
    constructor(private val foobar: Int) {
    fun foo(): String = "foo"
}
```

For other code styles (intellij_idea, android_studio), the existing behavior is preserved - annotations before constructors are allowed on the same line.

1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→WARNING: This version on KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgrade before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
  8→
  9→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to migrate to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 10→- The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI and `.editorconfig`.
 11→- The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 12→- The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 13→
 14→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 15→
 16→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 17→
 18→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 19→* The `.editorconfig` setting `disabled_rules`.
 20→* KtLint disable and enable directives.
 21→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 22→
 23→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
 24→
 25→#### Experimental rules
 26→
 27→Rules in custom rule sets can be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint whenever the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled.
 28→
 29→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set. A rule is identified by its qualified rule id that includes the rule set id. Moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set has the downside that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
 30→
 31→#### EditorConfig
 32→
 33→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. Note that also new fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
 34→
 35→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
 36→
 37→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
 38→
 39→A new code style with name `ktlint_offical` has been added. This code style makes it possible to innovate ktlint beyond current expectations of users that are familiar with the existing code styles. 
 40→
 41→The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
 42→
 43→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
 44→
 45→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
 46→
 47→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
 48→
 49→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
 50→
 51→This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
 52→

 46→/**
 47→ * Ensures that annotation are wrapped to separate lines.
 48→ *
 49→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#annotation-formatting
 50→ *
 51→ * 1) Place annotations on separate lines before the declaration to which they are attached, and with the same indentation:
 52→ *
 53→ *    ```
 54→ *    @Target(AnnotationTarget.PROPERTY)
 55→ *    annotation class JsonExclude
 56→ *    ```
 57→ *
 58→ * 2) Annotations without arguments may be placed on the same line:
 59→ *
 60→ *    ```
 61→ *    @JsonExclude @JvmField
 62→ *    var x: String
 63→ *    ```
 64→ *
 65→ * 3) A single annotation without arguments may be placed on the same line as the corresponding declaration:
 66→ *
 67→ *    ```
 68→ *    @Test fun foo() { /*...*/ }
 69→ *    ```
 70→ *
 71→ * 4) File annotations are placed after the file comment (if any), before the package statement, and are separated from package with a blank
 72→ *    line (to emphasize the fact that they target the file and not the package).
 73→ *
 74→ * @see [AnnotationSpacingRule] for white space rules.
 75→ */
 76→public class AnnotationRule :
 77→    StandardRule(
 78→        id = "annotation",
 79→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 80→            setOf(
 81→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 82→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 83→            ),
 84→        visitorModifiers =
 85→            setOf(
 86→                RunAfterRule(
 87→                    ruleId = ENUM_WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 88→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 89→                ),
 90→            ),
 91→    ) {
 92→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 93→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 94→
 95→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 96→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 97→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 98→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 99→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
100→        )
101→    }
102→
103→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
104→        node: ASTNode,
105→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
106→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
107→    ) {
108→        when (node.elementType) {
109→            FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST -> {
110→                visitFileAnnotationList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
111→            }
112→            ANNOTATION -> {
113→                // Annotation array
114→                //     @[...]
115→                visitAnnotation(node, emit, autoCorrect)
116→            }
117→            ANNOTATION_ENTRY -> {
118→                visitAnnotationEntry(node, emit, autoCorrect)
119→            }
120→        }
121→    }
122→
123→    private fun visitAnnotationEntry(
124→        node: ASTNode,
125→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
126→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
127→    ) {
128→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
129→
130→        if (node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() &&
131→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
132→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
133→            node.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
134→        ) {
135→            // Disallow:
136→            //     @Foo class Bar
137→            // Allow function parameters and arguments to have annotation on same line as identifier:
138→            //     fun @receiver:Bar String.foo() {}
139→            //     fun foo(
140→            //         @Bar("bar") bar,
141→            //         @Bar("bar") "42",
142→            //     ) {}
143→            checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
144→        }
145→
146→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()) {
147→            if (node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
148→                // Code below is disallowed
149→                //   @Foo1
150→                //   @Foo2 fun foo() {}
151→                emit(
152→                    node.startOffset,
153→                    "Annotation must be placed on a separate line when it is preceded by another annotation on a separate line",
154→                    true,
155→                )
156→                if (autoCorrect) {
157→                    node
158→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
159→                        .nextLeaf()
160→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
161→                }
162→            }
163→
164→            if (node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
165→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
166→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
167→                node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
168→                node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
169→            ) {
170→                // Code below is disallowed
171→                //   @Foo1 @Foo2 fun foo() {}
172→                // But following is allowed:
173→                //   @[Foo1 Foo2] fun foo() {}
174→                //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
175→                emit(
176→                    node.findAnnotatedConstruct().startOffset,
177→                    "Multiple annotations should not be placed on the same line as the annotated construct",
178→                    true,
179→                )
180→                if (autoCorrect) {
181→                    node
182→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
183→                        .nextCodeLeaf()
184→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
185→                            getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)
186→                                .applyIf(node.typeProjectionOrNull() != null) {
187→                                    plus(indentConfig.indent)
188→                                },
189→                        )
190→                }
191→            }
192→
193→            node.typeProjectionOrNull()
194→                ?.prevCodeLeaf()
195→                ?.let { startOfList ->
196→                    // Code below is disallowed
197→                    //   var foo: List<@Foo1 @Foo2 String>
198→                    // But following is allowed:
199→                    //   var foo: List<@[Foo1 Foo2] String>
200→                    //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
201→                    if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
202→                        node.isFirstAnnotationEntry()
203→                    ) {
204→                        emit(startOfList.startOffset, "Expected newline after '${startOfList.text}'", true)
205→                        if (autoCorrect) {
206→                            startOfList
207→                                .upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(
208→                                    getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).plus(indentConfig.indent),
209→                                )
210→                        }
211→                    }
212→                    if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
213→                        node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
214→                    ) {
215→                        node
216→                            .findAnnotatedConstruct()
217→                            .treeParent
218→                            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
219→                            .nextLeaf { it.isCodeLeaf() && it.elementType != ElementType.COMMA }
220→                            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
221→                                emit(codeLeaf.startOffset, "Expected newline before '${codeLeaf.text}'", true)
222→                                if (autoCorrect) {
223→                                    codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
224→                                }
225→                            }
226→                    }
227→                }
228→        }
229→
230→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() && node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
231→            // Code below is disallowed
232→            //   @Foo1
233→            //   @Foo2 @Foo3
234→            //   fun foo() {}
235→            emit(
236→                node.startOffset,
237→                "All annotations should either be on a single line or all annotations should be on a separate line",
238→                true,
239→            )
240→            if (autoCorrect) {
241→                node
242→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
243→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
244→            }
245→        }
246→    }
247→
248→    private fun ASTNode.typeProjectionOrNull() =
249→        takeIf { elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
250→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST }
251→            ?.treeParent
252→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE }
253→            ?.treeParent
254→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION }
255→            ?.treeParent
256→
257→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine(): Boolean {
258→        val firstAnnotation = treeParent.findChildByType(ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
259→        return siblings(forward = false)
260→            .takeWhile { it != firstAnnotation }
261→            .any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
262→    }
263→
264→    private fun checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
265→        node: ASTNode,
266→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
267→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
268→    ) {
269→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry()) {
270→            emit(
271→                node.startOffset,
272→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
273→                true,
274→            )
275→            if (autoCorrect) {
276→                node
277→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
278→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
279→            }
280→        }
281→
282→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct()) {
283→            emit(
284→                node.startOffset,
285→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
286→                // Annotated expressions for which the annotation contains a parameter can be hard to correct
287→                // automatically. See examples below. For now, let them be fixed manually.
288→                // fun foo1() = @Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()
289→                // if (@Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()) { .. }
290→                node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION,
291→            )
292→            if (autoCorrect) {
293→                node
294→                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
295→                    .nextLeaf()
296→                    .safeAs<LeafPsiElement>()
297→                    ?.let {
298→                        if (it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
299→                            it.replaceWithText(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
300→                        } else {
301→                            it.rawInsertBeforeMe(
302→                                PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)),
303→                            )
304→                        }
305→                    }
306→            }
307→        }
308→    }
309→
310→    private fun ASTNode.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation() = getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() != AnnotationUseSiteTarget.RECEIVER
311→
312→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() =
313→        psi
314→            .safeAs<KtAnnotationEntry>()
315→            ?.useSiteTarget
316→            ?.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget()
317→
318→    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() = getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() != null
319→
320→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() =
321→        takeIf { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
322→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST)
323→
324→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstAnnotationEntry() =
325→        this ==
326→            treeParent
327→                .children()
328→                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
329→
330→    private fun ASTNode.isLastAnnotationEntry() =
331→        this ==
332→            treeParent
333→                .children()
334→                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
335→
336→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
337→        siblings(forward = false)
338→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
339→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
340→
341→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
342→        siblings()
343→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
344→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
345→
346→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() =
347→        siblings(forward = false)
348→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
349→
350→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry() =
351→        siblings(forward = false)
352→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
353→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
354→
355→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() = siblings(forward = true).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
356→
357→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry() =
358→        siblings(forward = true)
359→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
360→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
361→
362→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(): Boolean {
363→        val annotatedConstruct = findAnnotatedConstruct()
364→        return lastChildLeafOrSelf()
365→            .leaves(forward = true)
366→            .takeWhile { it != annotatedConstruct }
367→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
368→    }
369→
370→    private fun ASTNode.findAnnotatedConstruct(): ASTNode {
371→        val astNode =
372→            if (treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST) {
373→                treeParent
374→            } else {
375→                this
376→            }
377→        return checkNotNull(
378→            astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextCodeLeaf(),
379→        )
380→    }
381→
382→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct() =
383→        if (isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
384→            isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()
385→        } else {
386→            isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
387→        }
388→
389→    private fun visitFileAnnotationList(
390→        node: ASTNode,
391→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
392→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
393→    ) {
394→        node
395→            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
396→            .nextCodeLeaf()
397→            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
398→                val whitespaceBefore = codeLeaf.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
399→
400→                if (whitespaceBefore == null || whitespaceBefore.text != "\n\n") {
401→                    emit(
402→                        codeLeaf.startOffset,
403→                        "File annotations should be separated from file contents with a blank line",
404→                        true,
405→                    )
406→                    if (autoCorrect) {
407→                        codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n\n")
408→                    }
409→                }
410→            }
411→    }
412→
413→    private fun visitAnnotation(
414→        node: ASTNode,
415→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
416→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
417→    ) {
418→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION)
419→
420→        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
421→            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry())
422→        ) {
423→            emit(
424→                node.startOffset,
425→                "@[...] style annotations should be on a separate line from other annotations.",
426→                true,
427→            )
428→            if (autoCorrect) {
429→                if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
430→                    node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
431→                } else if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
432→                    node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
433→                }
434→            }
435→        }
436→    }
437→
438→    private fun getNewlineWithIndent(modifierListRoot: ASTNode): String {
439→        val nodeBeforeAnnotations =
440→            modifierListRoot
441→                .treeParent
442→                .treePrev
443→        // Make sure we only insert a single newline
444→        val indentWithoutNewline =
445→            nodeBeforeAnnotations
446→                ?.text
447→                .orEmpty()
448→                .substringAfterLast('\n')
449→        return "\n".plus(indentWithoutNewline)
450→    }
451→}
452→
453→public val ANNOTATION_RULE_ID: RuleId = AnnotationRule().ruleId
454→

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules

  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.findCompositeParentElementOfType
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isPartOfCompositeElementOfType
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextSibling
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevSibling
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 13→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 14→
 15→/**
 16→ * Lints and formats the spacing before and after the angle brackets of a type argument list.
 17→ */
 18→public class TypeArgumentListSpacingRule :
 19→    StandardRule("type-argument-list-spacing"),
 20→    Rule.Experimental {
 21→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 22→        node: ASTNode,
 23→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 24→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 25→    ) {
 26→        when (node.elementType) {
 27→            ElementType.TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> {
 28→                visitFunctionDeclaration(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 29→                visitInsideTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 30→            }
 31→            ElementType.SUPER_TYPE_LIST, ElementType.SUPER_EXPRESSION ->
 32→                visitInsideTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 33→        }
 34→    }
 35→
 36→    private fun visitFunctionDeclaration(
 37→        node: ASTNode,
 38→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 39→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 40→    ) {
 41→        // No whitespace expected before type argument list of function call
 42→        //    val list = listOf <String>()
 43→        node
 44→            .prevLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
 45→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 46→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 47→
 48→        // No whitespace expected after type argument list of function call
 49→        //    val list = listOf<String> ()
 50→        node
 51→            .takeUnless {
 52→                // unless it is part of a type reference:
 53→                //    fun foo(): List<Foo> { ... }
 54→                //    var bar: List<Bar> = emptyList()
 55→                it.isPartOfCompositeElementOfType(ElementType.TYPE_REFERENCE)
 56→            }
 57→            ?.takeUnless {
 58→                // unless it is part of a call expression followed by lambda:
 59→                //    bar<Foo> { ... }
 60→                it.isPartOfCallExpressionFollowedByLambda()
 61→            }
 62→            ?.lastChildNode
 63→            ?.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
 64→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 65→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 66→    }
 67→
 68→    private fun visitInsideTypeArgumentList(
 69→        node: ASTNode,
 70→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 71→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 72→    ) {
 73→        // No whitespace expected after opening angle bracket of type argument list
 74→        //    val list = listOf< String>()
 75→        node
 76→            .findChildByType(ElementType.LT)
 77→            ?.nextSibling()
 78→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 79→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 80→
 81→        // No whitespace expected before closing angle bracket of type argument list
 82→        //    val list = listOf<String >()
 83→        node
 84→            .findChildByType(ElementType.GT)
 85→            ?.prevSibling()
 86→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 87→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 88→    }
 89→
 90→    private fun noWhitespaceExpected(
 91→        node: ASTNode,
 92→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 93→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 94→    ) {
 95→        if (node.text != "") {
 96→            emit(
 97→                node.startOffset,
 98→                "No whitespace expected at this position",
 99→                true,
100→            )
101→            if (autoCorrect) {
102→                node.treeParent.removeChild(node)
103→            }
104→        }
105→    }
106→}
107→
108→private fun ASTNode.isPartOfCallExpressionFollowedByLambda(): Boolean =
109→    findCompositeParentElementOfType(ElementType.CALL_EXPRESSION)
110→        ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.CALL_EXPRESSION }
111→        ?.findChildByType(ElementType.LAMBDA_ARGUMENT)
112→        .let { it != null }
113→
114→public val TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST_SPACING_RULE_ID: RuleId = TypeArgumentListSpacingRule().ruleId
115→

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.KtLintAssertThat.Companion.assertThatRule
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.LintViolation
  5→import org.junit.jupiter.api.Nested
  6→import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test
  7→
  8→class AnnotationRuleTest {
  9→    private val annotationRuleAssertThat = assertThatRule { AnnotationRule() }
 10→
 11→    @Test
 12→    fun `Given a single annotation on same line before the annotated construct`() {
 13→        val code =
 14→            """
 15→            @FunctionalInterface class FooBar {
 16→                @JvmField var foo: String
 17→                @Test fun bar() {}
 18→            }
 19→            """.trimIndent()
 20→        annotationRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
 21→    }
 22→
 23→    @Test
 24→    fun `Given a single annotation on line above the annotated construct`() {
 25→        val code =
 26→            """
 27→            @FunctionalInterface
 28→            class FooBar {
 29→                @JvmField
 30→                var foo: String
 31→                @Test
 32→                fun bar() {}
 33→            }
 34→            """.trimIndent()
 35→        annotationRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
 36→    }
 37→
 38→    @Test
 39→    fun `Given an annotation with a parameter followed by a EOL comment`() {
 40→        val code =
 41→            """
 42→            @Suppress("AnnotationRule") // some comment
 43→            class FooBar {
 44→                @Suppress("AnnotationRule") // some comment
 45→                var foo: String
 46→                @Suppress("AnnotationRule") // some comment
 47→                fun bar() {}
 48→            }
 49→            """.trimIndent()
 50→        annotationRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
 51→    }
 52→
 53→    @Test
 54→    fun `Given an annotation with a parameter on same line as annotation construct (possibly separated by a block comment or KDoc)`() {
 55→        val code =
 56→            """
 57→            @Suppress("AnnotationRule") class FooBar1 {
 58→                @Suppress("AnnotationRule") var foo: String
 59→                @Suppress("AnnotationRule") fun bar() {}
 60→            }
 61→            @Suppress("AnnotationRule") /* some comment */ class FooBar2 {
 62→                @Suppress("AnnotationRule") /* some comment */ var foo: String
 63→                @Suppress("AnnotationRule") /* some comment */ fun bar() {}
 64→            }
 65→            @Suppress("AnnotationRule") /** some comment */ class FooBar3 {
 66→                @Suppress("AnnotationRule") /** some comment */ var foo: String
 67→                @Suppress("AnnotationRule") /** some comment */ fun bar() {}
 68→            }
 69→            """.trimIndent()
 70→        val formattedCode =
 71→            """
 72→            @Suppress("AnnotationRule")
 73→            class FooBar1 {
 74→                @Suppress("AnnotationRule")
 75→                var foo: String
 76→                @Suppress("AnnotationRule")
 77→                fun bar() {}
 78→            }
 79→            @Suppress("AnnotationRule")
 80→            /* some comment */ class FooBar2 {
 81→                @Suppress("AnnotationRule")
 82→                /* some comment */ var foo: String
 83→                @Suppress("AnnotationRule")
 84→                /* some comment */ fun bar() {}
 85→            }
 86→            @Suppress("AnnotationRule")
 87→            /** some comment */ class FooBar3 {
 88→                @Suppress("AnnotationRule")
 89→                /** some comment */ var foo: String
 90→                @Suppress("AnnotationRule")
 91→                /** some comment */ fun bar() {}
 92→            }
 93→            """.trimIndent()
 94→        annotationRuleAssertThat(code)
 95→            .hasLintViolations(
 96→                LintViolation(1, 1, "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct"),
 97→                LintViolation(2, 5, "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct"),
 98→                LintViolation(3, 5, "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct"),
 99→                LintViolation(5, 1, "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct"),
100→                LintViolation(6, 5, "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct"),
101→                LintViolation(7, 5, "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct"),
102→                LintViolation(9, 1, "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct"),
103→                LintViolation(10, 5, "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct"),
104→                LintViolation(11, 5, "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct"),
105→            ).isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
106→    }
107→
108→    @Test
109→    fun `Given multiple annotations on same line as annotated construct`() {

111→            """
112→            @Foo @Bar class FooBar {
113→                @Foo @Bar var foo: String
114→                @Foo @Bar fun bar() {}
115→            }
116→            """.trimIndent()
117→        val formattedCode =

127→        annotationRuleAssertThat(code)

130→                LintViolation(2, 15, "Multiple annotations should not be placed on the same line as the annotated construct"),
131→                LintViolation(3, 15, "Multiple annotations should not be placed on the same line as the annotated construct"),
132→            ).isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
