 52→public class FunctionSignatureRule :
 53→    StandardRule(
 54→        id = "function-signature",
 55→        visitorModifiers =
 56→            setOf(
 57→                // Run after wrapping and spacing rules
 58→                VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 59→            ),
 60→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 61→            setOf(
 62→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 63→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 64→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 65→                FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY,
 66→                FUNCTION_BODY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_PROPERTY,
 67→            ),
 68→    ),
 69→    Rule.Experimental {
 70→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 71→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 72→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 73→    private var functionSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters =
 74→        FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 75→    private var functionBodyExpressionWrapping = FUNCTION_BODY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 76→
 77→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 78→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 79→        functionSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters = editorConfig[
 80→            FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY,
 81→        ]
 82→        functionBodyExpressionWrapping = editorConfig[FUNCTION_BODY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_PROPERTY]
 83→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 84→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 85→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 86→        )
 87→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 88→    }
 89→
 90→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 91→        node: ASTNode,
 92→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 93→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 94→    ) {
 95→        if (node.elementType == FUN) {
 96→            node
 97→                .functionSignatureNodes()
 98→                .any { it.elementType == EOL_COMMENT || it.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT }
 99→                .ifTrue {
100→                    // Rewriting function signatures in a consistent manner is hard or sometimes even impossible. For
101→                    // example a multiline signature which could fit on one line can not be rewritten in case it
102→                    // contains an EOL comment. Rewriting a single line signature which exceeds the max line length to a
103→                    // multiline signature is hard when it contains block comments. For now, it does not seem worth the
104→                    // effort to attempt it.
105→                    return
106→                }
107→
108→            visitFunctionSignature(node, emit, autoCorrect)
109→        }
110→    }
111→
112→    private fun ASTNode.functionSignatureNodes(): List<ASTNode> {
113→        // Find the signature including the element that has to be placed on the same line as the function signature
114→        //     fun foo(bar: String) {
115→        // or
116→        //     fun foo(bar: String) =
117→        val firstCodeChild = getFirstCodeChild()
118→        val startOfBodyBlock =
119→            this
120→                .findChildByType(BLOCK)
121→                ?.firstChildNode
122→        val startOfBodyExpression = this.findChildByType(EQ)
123→        return collectLeavesRecursively()
124→            .childrenBetween(
125→                startASTNodePredicate = { it == firstCodeChild },
126→                endASTNodePredicate = { it == startOfBodyBlock || it == startOfBodyExpression },
127→            )
128→    }
129→
130→    private fun ASTNode.getFirstCodeChild(): ASTNode? {
131→        val funNode =
132→            if (elementType == FUN_KEYWORD) {
133→                this.treeParent
134→            } else {
135→                this
136→            }
137→        funNode
138→            ?.findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
139→            ?.let { modifierList ->
140→                val iterator = modifierList.children().iterator()
141→                var currentNode: ASTNode
142→                while (iterator.hasNext()) {
143→                    currentNode = iterator.next()
144→                    if (currentNode.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
145→                        currentNode.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY &&
146→                        currentNode.elementType != WHITE_SPACE
147→                    ) {
148→                        return currentNode
149→                    }
150→                }
151→                return modifierList.nextCodeSibling()
152→            }
153→        return funNode.nextCodeLeaf()
154→    }
155→
156→    private fun visitFunctionSignature(
157→        node: ASTNode,
158→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
159→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
160→    ) {
161→        require(node.elementType == FUN)
162→
163→        val forceMultilineSignature =
164→            node.hasMinimumNumberOfParameters() ||
165→                node.containsMultilineParameter() ||
166→                (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.containsAnnotatedParameter())
167→        if (isMaxLineLengthSet()) {
168→            val singleLineFunctionSignatureLength = calculateFunctionSignatureLengthAsSingleLineSignature(node, emit, autoCorrect)
169→            // Function signatures not having parameters, should not be reformatted automatically. It would result in function signatures
170→            // like below, which are not acceptable:
171→            //     fun aVeryLongFunctionName(
172→            //     ) = "some-value"
173→            //
174→            //     fun aVeryLongFunctionName(
175→            //     ): SomeVeryLongTypeName =
176→            //         SomeVeryLongTypeName(...)
177→            // Leave it up to the max-line-length rule to detect those violations so that the developer can handle it manually.
178→            val rewriteFunctionSignatureWithParameters = node.countParameters() > 0 && singleLineFunctionSignatureLength > maxLineLength
179→            if (forceMultilineSignature || rewriteFunctionSignatureWithParameters) {
180→                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = true, dryRun = false)
181→                // Due to rewriting the function signature, the remaining length on the last line of the multiline
182→                // signature needs to be recalculated
183→                val lengthOfLastLine = recalculateRemainLengthForFirstLineOfBodyExpression(node)
184→                fixFunctionBody(node, emit, autoCorrect, maxLineLength - lengthOfLastLine)
185→            } else {
186→                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = false)
187→                fixFunctionBody(node, emit, autoCorrect, maxLineLength - singleLineFunctionSignatureLength)
188→            }
189→        } else {
190→            // When max line length is not set then keep it as single line function signature only when the original
191→            // signature already was a single line signature. Otherwise, rewrite the entire signature as a multiline
192→            // signature.
193→            val rewriteToSingleLineFunctionSignature =
194→                node
195→                    .functionSignatureNodes()
196→                    .none { it.textContains('\n') }
197→            if (!forceMultilineSignature && rewriteToSingleLineFunctionSignature) {
198→                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = false)
199→            } else {
200→                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = true, dryRun = false)
201→            }
202→        }
203→    }
204→
205→    private fun recalculateRemainLengthForFirstLineOfBodyExpression(node: ASTNode): Int {
206→        val closingParenthesis =
207→            node
208→                .findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
209→                ?.findChildByType(RPAR)
210→        val tailNodesOfFunctionSignature =
211→            node
212→                .functionSignatureNodes()
213→                .childrenBetween(
214→                    startASTNodePredicate = { it == closingParenthesis },
215→                    endASTNodePredicate = { false },
216→                )
217→
218→        return node.indent(false).length +
219→            tailNodesOfFunctionSignature.sumOf { it.text.length }
220→    }
221→
222→    private fun ASTNode.containsMultilineParameter(): Boolean =
223→        findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
224→            ?.children()
225→            .orEmpty()
226→            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
227→            .any { it.textContains('\n') }
228→
229→    private fun ASTNode.containsAnnotatedParameter(): Boolean =
230→        findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
231→            ?.children()
232→            .orEmpty()
233→            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
234→            .any { it.isAnnotated() }
235→
236→    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotated() =
237→        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
238→            ?.children()
239→            .orEmpty()
240→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
241→
242→    private fun calculateFunctionSignatureLengthAsSingleLineSignature(
243→        node: ASTNode,
244→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
245→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
246→    ): Int {
247→        val actualFunctionSignatureLength = node.getFunctionSignatureLength()
248→
249→        // Calculate the length of the function signature in case it would be rewritten as single line (and without a
250→        // maximum line length). The white space correction will be calculated via a dry run of the actual fix.
251→        return actualFunctionSignatureLength +
252→            // Calculate the white space correction in case the signature would be rewritten to a single line
253→            fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = true)
254→    }
255→
256→    private fun ASTNode.getFunctionSignatureLength() = indent(false).length + getFunctionSignatureNodesLength()
257→
258→    private fun ASTNode.getFunctionSignatureNodesLength() =
259→        functionSignatureNodes()
260→            .joinTextToString()
261→            .length
262→
263→    private fun fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(
264→        node: ASTNode,
265→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
266→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
267→        multiline: Boolean,
268→        dryRun: Boolean,
269→    ): Int {
270→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
271→
272→        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
273→        val firstParameterInList =
274→            valueParameterList
275→                .children()
276→                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
277→
278→        whiteSpaceCorrection += if (firstParameterInList == null) {
279→            // handle empty parameter list
280→            fixWhiteSpacesInEmptyValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, dryRun)
281→        } else {
282→            fixWhiteSpacesBeforeFirstParameterInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun) +
283→                fixWhiteSpacesBetweenParametersInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun) +
284→                fixWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun)
285→        }
286→
287→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
288→    }
289→
290→    private fun fixWhiteSpacesInEmptyValueParameterList(
291→        node: ASTNode,
292→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
293→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
294→        dryRun: Boolean,
295→    ): Int {
296→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
297→
298→        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
299→
300→        valueParameterList
301→            .children()
302→            .filter { it.elementType != LPAR && it.elementType != RPAR }
303→            .also { elementsInValueParameterList ->
304→                // Functions with comments in the value parameter list are excluded from processing before. So an "empty" value
305→                // parameter list should only contain a single whitespace element
306→                require(elementsInValueParameterList.count() <= 1)
307→            }
308→            .firstOrNull()
309→            ?.let { whiteSpace ->
310→                if (!dryRun) {
311→                    emit(
312→                        whiteSpace.startOffset,
313→                        "No whitespace expected in empty parameter list",
314→                        true,
315→                    )
316→                }
317→                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
318→                    whiteSpace.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpace)
319→                } else {
320→                    whiteSpaceCorrection -= whiteSpace.textLength
321→                }
322→            }
323→
324→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
325→    }
326→
327→    private fun fixWhiteSpacesBeforeFirstParameterInValueParameterList(
328→        node: ASTNode,
329→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
330→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
331→        multiline: Boolean,
332→        dryRun: Boolean,
333→    ): Int {
334→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
335→
336→        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
337→        val firstParameterInList =
338→            valueParameterList
339→                .children()
340→                .first { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
341→
342→        val firstParameter = firstParameterInList.firstChildNode
343→        firstParameter
344→            ?.prevLeaf()
345→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
346→            .let { whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier ->
347→                if (multiline) {
348→                    val expectedParameterIndent = node.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent)
349→                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
350→                        whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != expectedParameterIndent
351→                    ) {
352→                        if (!dryRun) {
353→                            emit(
354→                                firstParameterInList.startOffset,
355→                                "Newline expected after opening parenthesis",
356→                                true,
357→                            )
358→                        }
359→                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
360→                            valueParameterList.firstChildNode.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(expectedParameterIndent)
361→                        } else {
362→                            whiteSpaceCorrection += expectedParameterIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
363→                        }
364→                    }
365→                } else {
366→                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier != null) {
367→                        if (!dryRun) {
368→                            emit(
369→                                firstParameter!!.startOffset,
370→                                "No whitespace expected between opening parenthesis and first parameter name",
371→                                true,
372→                            )
373→                        }
374→                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
375→                            whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier)
376→                        } else {
377→                            whiteSpaceCorrection -= whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.textLength
378→                        }
379→                    }
380→                }
381→            }
382→
383→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
384→    }
385→
386→    private fun fixWhiteSpacesBetweenParametersInValueParameterList(
387→        node: ASTNode,
388→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
389→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
390→        multiline: Boolean,
391→        dryRun: Boolean,
392→    ): Int {
393→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
394→
395→        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
396→        val firstParameterInList =
397→            valueParameterList
398→                .children()
399→                .first { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
400→
401→        valueParameterList
402→            .children()
403→            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
404→            .filter { it != firstParameterInList }
405→            .forEach { valueParameter ->
406→                val firstChildNodeInValueParameter = valueParameter.firstChildNode
407→                firstChildNodeInValueParameter
408→                    ?.prevLeaf()
409→                    ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
410→                    .let { whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier ->
411→                        if (multiline) {
412→                            val expectedParameterIndent = node.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent)
413→                            if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
414→                                whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != expectedParameterIndent
415→                            ) {
416→                                if (!dryRun) {
417→                                    emit(
418→                                        valueParameter.startOffset,
419→                                        "Parameter should start on a newline",
420→                                        true,
421→                                    )
422→                                }
423→                                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
424→                                    firstChildNodeInValueParameter.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expectedParameterIndent)
425→                                } else {
426→                                    whiteSpaceCorrection += expectedParameterIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
427→                                }
428→                            }
429→                        } else {
430→                            if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null || whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != " ") {
431→                                if (!dryRun) {
432→                                    emit(
433→                                        firstChildNodeInValueParameter!!.startOffset,
434→                                        "Single whitespace expected before parameter",
435→                                        true,
436→                                    )
437→                                }
438→                                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
439→                                    firstChildNodeInValueParameter.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
440→                                } else {
441→                                    whiteSpaceCorrection += 1 - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
442→                                }
443→                            }
444→                        }
445→                    }
446→            }
447→
448→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
449→    }

451→    private fun fixWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis(
452→        node: ASTNode,
453→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
454→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
455→        multiline: Boolean,
456→        dryRun: Boolean,
457→    ): Int {
458→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
459→
460→        val newlineAndIndent = node.indent()
461→        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
462→
463→        val closingParenthesis = valueParameterList.findChildByType(RPAR)
464→        closingParenthesis
465→            ?.prevSibling()
466→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
467→            .let { whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis ->
468→                if (multiline) {
469→                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis == null ||
470→                        whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.text != newlineAndIndent
471→                    ) {
472→                        if (!dryRun) {
473→                            emit(
474→                                closingParenthesis!!.startOffset,
475→                                "Newline expected before closing parenthesis",
476→                                true,
477→                            )
478→                        }
479→                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
480→                            closingParenthesis!!.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(newlineAndIndent)
481→                        } else {
482→                            whiteSpaceCorrection += newlineAndIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis?.textLength ?: 0)
483→                        }
484→                    }
485→                } else {
486→                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis != null &&
487→                        whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.nextLeaf()?.elementType == RPAR
488→                    ) {
489→                        if (!dryRun) {
490→                            emit(
491→                                whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.startOffset,
492→                                "No whitespace expected between last parameter and closing parenthesis",
493→                                true,
494→                            )
495→                        }
496→                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
497→                            whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis)
498→                        } else {
499→                            whiteSpaceCorrection -= whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.textLength
500→                        }
501→                    }
502→                }
503→            }
504→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
505→    }

507→    private fun fixFunctionBody(
508→        node: ASTNode,
509→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
510→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
511→        maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression: Int,
512→    ) {
513→        if (node.findChildByType(EQ) == null) {
514→            fixFunctionBodyBlock(node, emit, autoCorrect)
515→        } else {
516→            fixFunctionBodyExpression(node, emit, autoCorrect, maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression)
517→        }
518→    }
519→
520→    private fun fixFunctionBodyExpression(
521→        node: ASTNode,
522→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
523→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
524→        maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression: Int,
525→    ) {
526→        val lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression =
527→            node
528→                .findChildByType(EQ)
529→                ?.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
530→                ?: return
531→        val bodyNodes = node.getFunctionBody(lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression)
532→        val whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression = bodyNodes.getStartingWhitespaceOrNull()
533→        val functionBodyExpressionNodes = bodyNodes.dropWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() }
534→
535→        val functionBodyExpressionLines =
536→            functionBodyExpressionNodes
537→                .joinTextToString()
538→                .split("\n")
539→        functionBodyExpressionLines
540→            .firstOrNull()
541→            ?.also { firstLineOfBodyExpression ->
542→                if (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
543→                    val mergeWithFunctionSignature =
544→                        if (firstLineOfBodyExpression.length < maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression) {
545→                            functionBodyExpressionWrapping == default ||
546→                                (functionBodyExpressionWrapping == multiline && functionBodyExpressionLines.size == 1) ||
547→                                node.isMultilineFunctionSignatureWithoutExplicitReturnType(
548→                                    lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression,
549→                                )
550→                        } else {
551→                            false
552→                        }
553→                    if (mergeWithFunctionSignature) {
554→                        emit(
555→                            whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression!!.startOffset,
556→                            "First line of body expression fits on same line as function signature",
557→                            true,
558→                        )
559→                        if (autoCorrect) {
560→                            (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(" ")
561→                        }
562→                    }
563→                } else if (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression == null ||
564→                    !whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression.textContains('\n')
565→                ) {
566→                    if (node.isMultilineFunctionSignatureWithoutExplicitReturnType(lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression) &&
567→                        firstLineOfBodyExpression.length + 1 <= maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression
568→                    ) {
569→                        if (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression == null ||
570→                            whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression.text != " "
571→                        ) {
572→                            emit(
573→                                functionBodyExpressionNodes.first().startOffset,
574→                                "Single whitespace expected before expression body",
575→                                true,
576→                            )
577→                            if (autoCorrect) {
578→                                functionBodyExpressionNodes
579→                                    .first()
580→                                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
581→                            }
582→                        }
583→                    } else if (firstLineOfBodyExpression.length + 1 > maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression ||
584→                        (functionBodyExpressionWrapping == multiline && functionBodyExpressionLines.size > 1) ||
585→                        functionBodyExpressionWrapping == always
586→                    ) {
587→                        emit(
588→                            functionBodyExpressionNodes.first().startOffset,
589→                            "Newline expected before expression body",
590→                            true,
591→                        )
592→                        if (autoCorrect) {
593→                            functionBodyExpressionNodes
594→                                .first()
595→                                .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(node.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
596→                        }
597→                    }
598→                }
599→            }
600→    }
601→
602→    private fun ASTNode.isMultilineFunctionSignatureWithoutExplicitReturnType(lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression: ASTNode?) =
603→        functionSignatureNodes()
604→            .childrenBetween(
605→                startASTNodePredicate = { true },
606→                endASTNodePredicate = { it == lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression },
607→            ).joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
608→            .split("\n")
609→            .lastOrNull()
610→            ?.matches(INDENT_WITH_CLOSING_PARENTHESIS)
611→            ?: false
612→
613→    private fun fixFunctionBodyBlock(
614→        node: ASTNode,
615→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
616→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
617→    ) {
618→        val lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBlockBody =
619→            node
620→                .getLastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBlockBody()
621→                ?.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
622→                ?: return
623→
624→        val bodyNodes = node.getFunctionBody(lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBlockBody)
625→        val whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression = bodyNodes.getStartingWhitespaceOrNull()
626→        val functionBodyBlock = bodyNodes.dropWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() }
627→
628→        functionBodyBlock
629→            .joinTextToString()
630→            .split("\n")
631→            .firstOrNull()
632→            ?.also {
633→                if (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression?.text != " ") {
634→                    emit(functionBodyBlock.first().startOffset, "Expected a single space before body block", true)
635→                    if (autoCorrect) {
636→                        functionBodyBlock
637→                            .first()
638→                            .prevLeaf(true)
639→                            ?.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
640→                    }
641→                }
642→            }
643→    }

645→    private fun ASTNode.getFunctionBody(splitNode: ASTNode?): List<ASTNode> =
646→        this
647→            .collectLeavesRecursively()
648→            .childrenBetween(
649→                startASTNodePredicate = { it == splitNode },
650→                endASTNodePredicate = {
651→                    // collect all remaining nodes
652→                    false
653→                },
654→            )
655→
656→    private fun List<ASTNode>.getStartingWhitespaceOrNull() =
657→        this
658→            .firstOrNull()
659→            ?.takeIf { first -> first.isWhiteSpace() }

661→    private fun isMaxLineLengthSet() = maxLineLength != MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF

663→    private fun List<ASTNode>.collectLeavesRecursively(): List<ASTNode> = flatMap { it.collectLeavesRecursively() }
664→
665→    private fun ASTNode.collectLeavesRecursively(): List<ASTNode> =
666→        if (psi is LeafElement) {
667→            listOf(this)
668→        } else {
669→            children()
670→                .flatMap { it.collectLeavesRecursively() }
671→                .toList()
672→        }
673→
674→    private fun List<ASTNode>.childrenBetween(
675→        startASTNodePredicate: (ASTNode) -> Boolean = { _ -> true },
676→        endASTNodePredicate: (ASTNode) -> Boolean = { _ -> false },
677→    ): List<ASTNode> {
678→        val iterator = iterator()
679→        var currentNode: ASTNode
680→        val childrenBetween: MutableList<ASTNode> = mutableListOf()
681→
682→        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
683→            currentNode = iterator.next()
684→            if (startASTNodePredicate(currentNode)) {
685→                childrenBetween.add(currentNode)
686→                break
687→            }
688→        }
689→
690→        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
691→            currentNode = iterator.next()
692→            childrenBetween.add(currentNode)
693→            if (endASTNodePredicate(currentNode)) {
694→                break
695→            }
696→        }
697→
698→        return childrenBetween
699→    }
700→
701→    private fun List<ASTNode>.joinTextToString(block: (ASTNode) -> String = { it.text }): String =
702→        collectLeavesRecursively().joinToString(separator = "") { block(it) }
703→
704→    private fun ASTNode.hasMinimumNumberOfParameters(): Boolean = countParameters() >= functionSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters
705→
706→    private fun ASTNode.countParameters(): Int {
707→        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
708→        return valueParameterList
709→            .children()
710→            .count { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
711→    }

713→    private fun ASTNode.getLastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBlockBody(): ASTNode? =
714→        this
715→            .findChildByType(BLOCK)
716→            ?.firstChildNode
717→            ?.prevCodeLeaf()

719→    public companion object {
720→        public val FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<Int> =
721→            EditorConfigProperty(
722→                type =
723→                    PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
724→                        "ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than",
725→                        "Force wrapping the parameters of the function signature in case it contains at least the specified " +
726→                            "number of parameters even in case the entire function signature would fit on a single line. " +
727→                            "By default this parameter is not enabled.",
728→                        PropertyType.PropertyValueParser.POSITIVE_INT_VALUE_PARSER,
729→                        setOf("1", "2", "3", "4", "5", "6", "7", "8", "9", "unset"),
730→                    ),
731→                defaultValue = Int.MAX_VALUE,
732→                ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = 2,
733→            )
734→
735→        public val FUNCTION_BODY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<FunctionBodyExpressionWrapping> =
736→            EditorConfigProperty(
737→                type =
738→                    PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
739→                        "ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping",
740→                        "Determines how to wrap the body of function in case it is an expression. Use 'default' " +
741→                            "to wrap the body expression only when the first line of the expression does not fit on the same " +
742→                            "line as the function signature. Use 'multiline' to force wrapping of body expressions that " +
743→                            "consists of multiple line. Use 'always' to force wrapping of body expression always.",
744→                        EnumValueParser(FunctionBodyExpressionWrapping::class.java),
745→                        FunctionBodyExpressionWrapping.values().map { it.name }.toSet(),
746→                    ),
747→                defaultValue = default,
748→                ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = multiline,
749→            )
750→
751→        private val INDENT_WITH_CLOSING_PARENTHESIS = Regex("\\s*\\) =")
752→    }

754→    /**
755→     * Code style to be used while linting and formatting. Note that the [EnumValueParser] requires values to be lowercase.
756→     */
757→    @Suppress("EnumEntryName")
758→    public enum class FunctionBodyExpressionWrapping {
759→        /**
760→         * Keep the first line of the body expression on the same line as the function signature if max line length is
761→         * not exceeded.
762→         */
763→        default,
764→
765→        /**
766→         * Force the body expression to start on a separate line in case it is a multiline expression. A single line
767→         * body expression is wrapped only when it does not fit on the same line as the function signature.
768→         */
769→        multiline,
770→
771→        /**
772→         * Always force the body expression to start on a separate line.
773→         */
774→        always,
775→    }

 6→/**
 7→ * Code style to be used while linting and formatting. Note that the [EnumValueParser] requires values to be lowercase.
 8→ */
 9→@Suppress("EnumEntryName")
10→public enum class CodeStyleValue {
11→    @Deprecated(
12→        message = "Marked for removal in KtLint 0.50. Value is renamed to 'android_studio'.",
13→        replaceWith = ReplaceWith("android_studio"),
14→    )
15→    android,
16→
17→    /**
18→     * Code formatting based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This
19→     * code style aims to be compatible with code formatting in Android Studio.
20→     */
21→    android_studio,
22→
23→    /**
24→     * Code formatting based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). This
25→     * code style aims to be compatible with code formatting in IntelliJ IDEA.
26→     */
27→    intellij_idea,
28→
29→    /**
30→     * Code formatting based on the best of both the Kotlin Coding conventions
31→     * (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide
32→     * (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics
33→     * which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats
34→     * code in a way which is not compatible with the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. When
35→     * using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) automatic code formatting in the editor. Mean reason
36→     * for deviating from the code formatting provided by the editor is that those contain bugs which after some years
37→     * are still not fixed.
38→     * In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
39→     */
40→    ktlint_official,
41→
42→    @Deprecated(
43→        message = "Marked for removal in KtLint 0.50. Value is renamed to 'intellij_idea'.",
44→        replaceWith = ReplaceWith("intellij_idea"),
45→    )
46→    official,
47→}

49→public val CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY_TYPE: PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType<CodeStyleValue> =
50→    PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
51→        "ktlint_code_style",
52→        "The code style ('intellij_idea', 'android_studio' or 'ktlint_official') to be applied. Currently, the " +
53→            "value is defaulted to 'intellij_idea' when not set. However, in the future the default code style " +
54→            "will be changed to 'ktlint_official'.",
55→        SafeEnumValueParser(CodeStyleValue::class.java),
56→        CodeStyleValue.values().map { it.name }.toSet(),
57→    )

62→        /**

64→         * then the [CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea] is used to remain backwards compatible.
65→         */
66→        defaultValue = CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea,
67→        androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue = CodeStyleValue.android_studio,
68→        intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue = CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea,
69→        ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official,
70→    )

 38→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 39→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiWhiteSpaceImpl
 40→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.descriptors.annotations.AnnotationUseSiteTarget
 41→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtAnnotationEntry
 42→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.leaves
 43→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.siblings
 44→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.utils.addToStdlib.applyIf
 45→
 46→/**
 47→ * Ensures that annotation are wrapped to separate lines.
 48→ *
 49→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#annotation-formatting
 50→ *
 51→ * 1) Place annotations on separate lines before the declaration to which they are attached, and with the same indentation:
 52→ *
 53→ *    ```
 54→ *    @Target(AnnotationTarget.PROPERTY)
 55→ *    annotation class JsonExclude
 56→ *    ```
 57→ *
 58→ * 2) Annotations without arguments may be placed on the same line:
 59→ *
 60→ *    ```
 61→ *    @JsonExclude @JvmField
 62→ *    var x: String
 63→ *    ```
 64→ *
 65→ * 3) A single annotation without arguments may be placed on the same line as the corresponding declaration:
 66→ *
 67→ *    ```
 68→ *    @Test fun foo() { /*...*/ }
 69→ *    ```
 70→ *
 71→ * 4) File annotations are placed after the file comment (if any), before the package statement, and are separated from package with a blank
 72→ *    line (to emphasize the fact that they target the file and not the package).
 73→ *
 74→ * @see [AnnotationSpacingRule] for white space rules.
 75→ */
 76→public class AnnotationRule :
 77→    StandardRule(
 78→        id = "annotation",
 79→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 80→            setOf(
 81→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 82→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 83→            ),
 84→        visitorModifiers =
 85→            setOf(
 86→                RunAfterRule(
 87→                    ruleId = ENUM_WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 88→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 89→                ),
 90→            ),
 91→    ) {
 92→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 93→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 94→
 95→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 96→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 97→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 98→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 99→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
100→        )
101→    }
102→
103→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
104→        node: ASTNode,
105→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
106→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
107→    ) {
108→        when (node.elementType) {
109→            FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST -> {
110→                visitFileAnnotationList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
111→            }
112→            ANNOTATION -> {
113→                // Annotation array
114→                //     @[...]
115→                visitAnnotation(node, emit, autoCorrect)
116→            }
117→            ANNOTATION_ENTRY -> {
118→                visitAnnotationEntry(node, emit, autoCorrect)
119→            }
120→        }
121→    }
122→
123→    private fun visitAnnotationEntry(
124→        node: ASTNode,
125→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
126→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
127→    ) {
128→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
129→
130→        if (node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() &&
131→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
132→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
133→            node.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
134→        ) {
135→            // Disallow:
136→            //     @Foo class Bar
137→            // Allow function parameters and arguments to have annotation on same line as identifier:
138→            //     fun @receiver:Bar String.foo() {}
139→            //     fun foo(
140→            //         @Bar("bar") bar,
141→            //         @Bar("bar") "42",
142→            //     ) {}
143→            checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
144→        }
145→
146→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()) {
147→            if (node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
148→                // Code below is disallowed
149→                //   @Foo1
150→                //   @Foo2 fun foo() {}
151→                emit(
152→                    node.startOffset,
153→                    "Annotation must be placed on a separate line when it is preceded by another annotation on a separate line",
154→                    true,
155→                )
156→                if (autoCorrect) {
157→                    node
158→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
159→                        .nextLeaf()
160→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
161→                }
162→            }
163→
164→            if (node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
165→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
166→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
167→                node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
168→                node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
169→            ) {
170→                // Code below is disallowed
171→                //   @Foo1 @Foo2 fun foo() {}
172→                // But following is allowed:
173→                //   @[Foo1 Foo2] fun foo() {}
174→                //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
175→                emit(
176→                    node.findAnnotatedConstruct().startOffset,
177→                    "Multiple annotations should not be placed on the same line as the annotated construct",
178→                    true,
179→                )
180→                if (autoCorrect) {
181→                    node
182→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
183→                        .nextCodeLeaf()
184→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
185→                            getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)
186→                                .applyIf(node.typeProjectionOrNull() != null) {
187→                                    plus(indentConfig.indent)
188→                                },
189→                        )
190→                }
191→            }
192→
193→            node.typeProjectionOrNull()
194→                ?.prevCodeLeaf()
195→                ?.let { startOfList ->
196→                    // Code below is disallowed
197→                    //   var foo: List<@Foo1 @Foo2 String>
198→                    // But following is allowed:
199→                    //   var foo: List<@[Foo1 Foo2] String>
200→                    //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
201→                    if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
202→                        node.isFirstAnnotationEntry()
203→                    ) {
204→                        emit(startOfList.startOffset, "Expected newline after '${startOfList.text}'", true)
205→                        if (autoCorrect) {
206→                            startOfList
207→                                .upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(
208→                                    getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).plus(indentConfig.indent),
209→                                )
210→                        }
211→                    }
212→                    if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
213→                        node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
214→                    ) {
215→                        node
216→                            .findAnnotatedConstruct()
217→                            .treeParent
218→                            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
219→                            .nextLeaf { it.isCodeLeaf() && it.elementType != ElementType.COMMA }
220→                            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
221→                                emit(codeLeaf.startOffset, "Expected newline before '${codeLeaf.text}'", true)
222→                                if (autoCorrect) {
223→                                    codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
224→                                }
225→                            }
226→                    }
227→                }
228→        }
229→
230→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() && node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
231→            // Code below is disallowed
232→            //   @Foo1
233→            //   @Foo2 @Foo3
234→            //   fun foo() {}
235→            emit(
236→                node.startOffset,
237→                "All annotations should either be on a single line or all annotations should be on a separate line",
238→                true,
239→            )
240→            if (autoCorrect) {
241→                node
242→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
243→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
244→            }
245→        }
246→    }
247→
248→    private fun ASTNode.typeProjectionOrNull() =
249→        takeIf { elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
250→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST }
251→            ?.treeParent
252→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE }
253→            ?.treeParent
254→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION }
255→            ?.treeParent
256→
257→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine(): Boolean {
258→        val firstAnnotation = treeParent.findChildByType(ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
259→        return siblings(forward = false)
260→            .takeWhile { it != firstAnnotation }
261→            .any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
262→    }
263→
264→    private fun checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
265→        node: ASTNode,
266→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
267→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
268→    ) {
269→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry()) {
270→            emit(
271→                node.startOffset,
272→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
273→                true,
274→            )
275→            if (autoCorrect) {
276→                node
277→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
278→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
279→            }
280→        }
281→
282→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct()) {
283→            emit(
284→                node.startOffset,
285→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
286→                // Annotated expressions for which the annotation contains a parameter can be hard to correct
287→                // automatically. See examples below. For now, let them be fixed manually.
288→                // fun foo1() = @Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()
289→                // if (@Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()) { .. }
290→                node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION,
291→            )
292→            if (autoCorrect) {
293→                node
294→                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
295→                    .nextLeaf()
296→                    .safeAs<LeafPsiElement>()
297→                    ?.let {
298→                        if (it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
299→                            it.replaceWithText(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
300→                        } else {
301→                            it.rawInsertBeforeMe(
302→                                PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)),
303→                            )
304→                        }
305→                    }
306→            }
307→        }
308→    }
309→
310→    private fun ASTNode.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation() = getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() != AnnotationUseSiteTarget.RECEIVER
311→
312→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() =
313→        psi
314→            .safeAs<KtAnnotationEntry>()
315→            ?.useSiteTarget
316→            ?.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget()
317→
318→    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() = getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() != null
319→
320→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() =
321→        takeIf { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
322→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST)
323→
324→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstAnnotationEntry() =
325→        this ==
326→            treeParent
327→                .children()
328→                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
329→
330→    private fun ASTNode.isLastAnnotationEntry() =
331→        this ==
332→            treeParent
333→                .children()
334→                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
335→
336→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
337→        siblings(forward = false)
338→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
339→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
340→
341→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
342→        siblings()
343→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
344→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
345→
346→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() =
347→        siblings(forward = false)
348→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
349→
350→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry() =
351→        siblings(forward = false)
352→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
353→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
354→
355→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() = siblings(forward = true).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
356→
357→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry() =
358→        siblings(forward = true)
359→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
360→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
361→
362→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(): Boolean {
363→        val annotatedConstruct = findAnnotatedConstruct()
364→        return lastChildLeafOrSelf()
365→            .leaves(forward = true)
366→            .takeWhile { it != annotatedConstruct }
367→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
368→    }
369→
370→    private fun ASTNode.findAnnotatedConstruct(): ASTNode {
371→        val astNode =
372→            if (treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST) {
373→                treeParent
374→            } else {
375→                this
376→            }
377→        return checkNotNull(
378→            astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextCodeLeaf(),
379→        )
380→    }
381→
382→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct() =
383→        if (isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
384→            isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()
385→        } else {
386→            isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
387→        }
388→
389→    private fun visitFileAnnotationList(
390→        node: ASTNode,
391→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
392→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
393→    ) {
394→        node
395→            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
396→            .nextCodeLeaf()
397→            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
398→                val whitespaceBefore = codeLeaf.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
399→
400→                if (whitespaceBefore == null || whitespaceBefore.text != "\n\n") {
401→                    emit(
402→                        codeLeaf.startOffset,
403→                        "File annotations should be separated from file contents with a blank line",
404→                        true,
405→                    )
406→                    if (autoCorrect) {
407→                        codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n\n")
408→                    }
409→                }
410→            }
411→    }
412→
413→    private fun visitAnnotation(
414→        node: ASTNode,
415→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
416→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
417→    ) {
418→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION)
419→
420→        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
421→            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry())
422→        ) {
423→            emit(
424→                node.startOffset,
425→                "@[...] style annotations should be on a separate line from other annotations.",
426→                true,
427→            )
428→            if (autoCorrect) {
429→                if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
430→                    node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
431→                } else if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
432→                    node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
433→                }
434→            }
435→        }
436→    }
437→
438→    private fun getNewlineWithIndent(modifierListRoot: ASTNode): String {
439→        val nodeBeforeAnnotations =
440→            modifierListRoot
441→                .treeParent
442→                .treePrev
443→        // Make sure we only insert a single newline
444→        val indentWithoutNewline =
445→            nodeBeforeAnnotations
446→                ?.text
447→                .orEmpty()
448→                .substringAfterLast('\n')
449→        return "\n".plus(indentWithoutNewline)
450→    }
451→}
452→
453→public val ANNOTATION_RULE_ID: RuleId = AnnotationRule().ruleId

 49→/**
 50→ * Ensures that annotation are wrapped to separate lines.
 51→ *
 52→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#annotation-formatting
 53→ *
 54→ * 1) Place annotations on separate lines before the declaration to which they are attached, and with the same indentation:
 55→ *
 56→ *    ```
 57→ *    @Target(AnnotationTarget.PROPERTY)
 58→ *    annotation class JsonExclude
 59→ *    ```
 60→ *
 61→ * 2) Annotations without arguments may be placed on the same line:
 62→ *
 63→ *    ```
 64→ *    @JsonExclude @JvmField
 65→ *    var x: String
 66→ *    ```
 67→ *
 68→ * 3) A single annotation without arguments may be placed on the same line as the corresponding declaration:
 69→ *
 70→ *    ```
 71→ *    @Test fun foo() { /*...*/ }
 72→ *    ```
 73→ *
 74→ * 4) File annotations are placed after the file comment (if any), before the package statement, and are separated from package with a blank
 75→ *    line (to emphasize the fact that they target the file and not the package).
 76→ *
 77→ * @see [AnnotationSpacingRule] for white space rules.
 78→ */
 79→public class AnnotationRule :
 80→    StandardRule(
 81→        id = "annotation",
 82→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 83→            setOf(
 84→                CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 85→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 86→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 87→            ),
 88→        visitorModifiers =
 89→            setOf(
 90→                RunAfterRule(
 91→                    ruleId = ENUM_WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 92→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 93→                ),
 94→            ),
 95→    ) {
 96→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 97→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 98→
 99→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
100→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
101→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
102→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
103→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
104→        )
105→    }
106→
107→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
108→        node: ASTNode,
109→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
110→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
111→    ) {
112→        when (node.elementType) {
113→            FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST -> {
114→                visitFileAnnotationList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
115→            }
116→            ANNOTATION -> {
117→                // Annotation array
118→                //     @[...]
119→                visitAnnotation(node, emit, autoCorrect)
120→            }
121→            ANNOTATION_ENTRY -> {
122→                visitAnnotationEntry(node, emit, autoCorrect)
123→            }
124→        }
125→    }
126→
127→    private fun visitAnnotationEntry(
128→        node: ASTNode,
129→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
130→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
131→    ) {
132→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
133→
134→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.isPrimaryConstructorAnnotation()) {
135→            visitPrimaryConstructorAnnotation(node, emit, autoCorrect)
136→            return
137→        }
138→
139→        if (node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() &&
140→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
141→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
142→            node.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
143→        ) {
144→            // Disallow:
145→            //     @Foo class Bar
146→            // Allow function parameters and arguments to have annotation on same line as identifier:
147→            //     fun @receiver:Bar String.foo() {}
148→            //     fun foo(
149→            //         @Bar("bar") bar,
150→            //         @Bar("bar") "42",
151→            //     ) {}
152→            checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
153→        }
154→
155→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()) {
156→            if (node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
157→                // Code below is disallowed
158→                //   @Foo1
159→                //   @Foo2 fun foo() {}
160→                emit(
161→                    node.startOffset,
162→                    "Annotation must be placed on a separate line when it is preceded by another annotation on a separate line",
163→                    true,
164→                )
165→                if (autoCorrect) {
166→                    node
167→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
168→                        .nextLeaf()
169→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
170→                }
171→            }
172→
173→            if (node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
174→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
175→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
176→                node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
177→                node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
178→            ) {
179→                // Code below is disallowed
180→                //   @Foo1 @Foo2 fun foo() {}
181→                // But following is allowed:
182→                //   @[Foo1 Foo2] fun foo() {}
183→                //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
184→                emit(
185→                    node.findAnnotatedConstruct().startOffset,
186→                    "Multiple annotations should not be placed on the same line as the annotated construct",
187→                    true,
188→                )
189→                if (autoCorrect) {
190→                    node
191→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
192→                        .nextCodeLeaf()
193→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
194→                            getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)
195→                                .applyIf(node.typeProjectionOrNull() != null) {
196→                                    plus(indentConfig.indent)
197→                                },
198→                        )
199→                }
200→            }
201→
202→            node.typeProjectionOrNull()
203→                ?.prevCodeLeaf()
204→                ?.let { startOfList ->
205→                    // Code below is disallowed
206→                    //   var foo: List<@Foo1 @Foo2 String>
207→                    // But following is allowed:
208→                    //   var foo: List<@[Foo1 Foo2] String>
209→                    //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
210→                    if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
211→                        node.isFirstAnnotationEntry()
212→                    ) {
213→                        emit(startOfList.startOffset, "Expected newline after '${startOfList.text}'", true)
214→                        if (autoCorrect) {
215→                            startOfList
216→                                .upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(
217→                                    getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).plus(indentConfig.indent),
218→                                )
219→                        }
220→                    }
221→                    if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
222→                        node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
223→                    ) {
224→                        node
225→                            .findAnnotatedConstruct()
226→                            .treeParent
227→                            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
228→                            .nextLeaf { it.isCodeLeaf() && it.elementType != ElementType.COMMA }
229→                            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
230→                                emit(codeLeaf.startOffset, "Expected newline before '${codeLeaf.text}'", true)
231→                                if (autoCorrect) {
232→                                    codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
233→                                }
234→                            }
235→                    }
236→                }
237→        }
238→
239→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() && node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
240→            // Code below is disallowed
241→            //   @Foo1
242→            //   @Foo2 @Foo3
243→            //   fun foo() {}
244→            emit(
245→                node.startOffset,
246→                "All annotations should either be on a single line or all annotations should be on a separate line",
247→                true,
248→            )
249→            if (autoCorrect) {
250→                node
251→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
252→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
253→            }
254→        }
255→    }
256→
257→    private fun ASTNode.typeProjectionOrNull() =
258→        takeIf { elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
259→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST }
260→            ?.treeParent
261→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE }
262→            ?.treeParent
263→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION }
264→            ?.treeParent
265→
266→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine(): Boolean {
267→        val firstAnnotation = treeParent.findChildByType(ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
268→        return siblings(forward = false)
269→            .takeWhile { it != firstAnnotation }
270→            .any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
271→    }
272→
273→    private fun checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
274→        node: ASTNode,
275→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
276→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
277→    ) {
278→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry()) {
279→            emit(
280→                node.startOffset,
281→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
282→                true,
283→            )
284→            if (autoCorrect) {
285→                node
286→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
287→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
288→            }
289→        }
290→
291→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct()) {
292→            emit(
293→                node.startOffset,
294→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
295→                // Annotated expressions for which the annotation contains a parameter can be hard to correct
296→                // automatically. See examples below. For now, let them be fixed manually.
297→                // fun foo1() = @Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()
298→                // if (@Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()) { .. }
299→                node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION,
300→            )
301→            if (autoCorrect) {
302→                node
303→                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
304→                    .nextLeaf()
305→                    .safeAs<LeafPsiElement>()
306→                    ?.let {
307→                        if (it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
308→                            it.replaceWithText(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
309→                        } else {
310→                            it.rawInsertBeforeMe(
311→                                PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)),
312→                            )
313→                        }
314→                    }
315→            }
316→        }
317→    }
318→
319→    private fun ASTNode.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation() = getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() != AnnotationUseSiteTarget.RECEIVER
320→
321→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() =
322→        psi
323→            .safeAs<KtAnnotationEntry>()
324→            ?.useSiteTarget
325→            ?.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget()
326→
327→    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() = getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() != null
328→
329→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() =
330→        takeIf { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
331→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST)
332→
333→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstAnnotationEntry() =
334→        this ==
335→            treeParent
336→                .children()
337→                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
338→
339→    private fun ASTNode.isLastAnnotationEntry() =
340→        this ==
341→            treeParent
342→                .children()
343→                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
344→
345→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
346→        siblings(forward = false)
347→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
348→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
349→
350→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
351→        siblings()
352→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
353→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
354→
355→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() =
356→        siblings(forward = false)
357→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
358→
359→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry() =
360→        siblings(forward = false)
361→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
362→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
363→
364→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() = siblings(forward = true).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
365→
366→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry() =
367→        siblings(forward = true)
368→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
369→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
370→
371→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(): Boolean {
372→        val annotatedConstruct = findAnnotatedConstruct()
373→        return lastChildLeafOrSelf()
374→            .leaves(forward = true)
375→            .takeWhile { it != annotatedConstruct }
376→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
377→    }
378→
379→    private fun ASTNode.findAnnotatedConstruct(): ASTNode {
380→        val astNode =
381→            if (treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST) {
382→                treeParent
383→            } else {
384→                this
385→            }
386→        return checkNotNull(
387→            astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextCodeLeaf(),
388→        )
389→    }
390→
391→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct() =
392→        if (isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
393→            isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()
394→        } else {
395→            isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
396→        }
397→
398→    private fun visitFileAnnotationList(
399→        node: ASTNode,
400→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
401→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
402→    ) {
403→        node
404→            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
405→            .nextCodeLeaf()
406→            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
407→                val whitespaceBefore = codeLeaf.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
408→
409→                if (whitespaceBefore == null || whitespaceBefore.text != "\n\n") {
410→                    emit(
411→                        codeLeaf.startOffset,
412→                        "File annotations should be separated from file contents with a blank line",
413→                        true,
414→                    )
415→                    if (autoCorrect) {
416→                        codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n\n")
417→                    }
418→                }
419→            }
420→    }
421→
422→    private fun visitAnnotation(
423→        node: ASTNode,
424→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
425→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
426→    ) {
427→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION)
428→
429→        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
430→            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry())
431→        ) {
432→            emit(
433→                node.startOffset,
434→                "@[...] style annotations should be on a separate line from other annotations.",
435→                true,
436→            )
437→            if (autoCorrect) {
438→                if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
439→                    node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
440→                } else if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
441→                    node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
442→                }
443→            }
444→        }
445→    }
446→
447→    private fun getNewlineWithIndent(modifierListRoot: ASTNode): String {
448→        val nodeBeforeAnnotations =
449→            modifierListRoot
450→                .treeParent
451→                .treePrev
452→        // Make sure we only insert a single newline
453→        val indentWithoutNewline =
454→            nodeBeforeAnnotations
455→                ?.text
456→                .orEmpty()
457→                .substringAfterLast('\n')
458→        return "\n".plus(indentWithoutNewline)
459→    }
460→}

 49→/**
 50→ * Ensures that annotation are wrapped to separate lines.
 51→ *
 52→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#annotation-formatting
 53→ *
 54→ * 1) Place annotations on separate lines before the declaration to which they are attached, and with the same indentation:
 55→ *
 56→ *    ```
 57→ *    @Target(AnnotationTarget.PROPERTY)
 58→ *    annotation class JsonExclude
 59→ *    ```
 60→ *
 61→ * 2) Annotations without arguments may be placed on the same line:
 62→ *
 63→ *    ```
 64→ *    @JsonExclude @JvmField
 65→ *    var x: String
 66→ *    ```
 67→ *
 68→ * 3) A single annotation without arguments may be placed on the same line as the corresponding declaration:
 69→ *
 70→ *    ```
 71→ *    @Test fun foo() { /*...*/ }
 72→ *    ```
 73→ *
 74→ * 4) File annotations are placed after the file comment (if any), before the package statement, and are separated from package with a blank
 75→ *    line (to emphasize the fact that they target the file and not the package).
 76→ *
 77→ * @see [AnnotationSpacingRule] for white space rules.
 78→ */

 81→        id = "annotation",

 85→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 86→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 87→            ),
 88→        visitorModifiers =
 89→            setOf(
 90→                RunAfterRule(
 91→                    ruleId = ENUM_WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 92→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 93→                ),
 94→            ),
 95→    ) {

107→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
108→        node: ASTNode,

113→            FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST -> {

260→            ?.treeParent
261→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE }

266→    private fun ASTNode.isPrimaryConstructorAnnotation() =
267→        takeIf { elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
268→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST }
269→            ?.treeParent
270→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR }
271→            .let { it != null }
272→
273→    private fun visitPrimaryConstructorAnnotation(
274→        node: ASTNode,
275→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
276→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
277→    ) {
278→        val expectedIndent = getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).plus(indentConfig.indent)
279→        if (node.isFirstAnnotationEntry()) {
280→            // Annotation before constructor should be on separate line from class identifier:
281→            //    data class Foo @Bar1 constructor()
282→            // should be formatted as:
283→            //    data class Foo
284→            //        @Bar1
285→            //        constructor()
286→            node
287→                .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
288→                .prevLeaf()
289→                ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE && !it.textContains('\n') }
290→                ?.let {
291→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Expected newline before annotation", true)
292→                    if (autoCorrect) {
293→                        node.firstChildLeafOrSelf().upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expectedIndent)
294→                    }
295→                }
296→        }
297→        if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine()) {
298→            // Each annotation should be on a separate line:
299→            //    data class Foo @Bar1 @Bar2 constructor()
300→            // should be formatted as:
301→            //    data class Foo
302→            //        @Bar1
303→            //        @Bar2
304→            //        constructor()
305→            emit(node.startOffset, "Expected each annotation to be on a separate line", true)
306→            if (autoCorrect) {
307→                node.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextLeaf()?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expectedIndent)
308→            }
309→        }
310→        if (node.isLastAnnotationEntry()) {
311→            // Last annotation should be on separate line from constructor keyword:
312→            //    data class Foo @Bar1 constructor()
313→            // should be formatted as:
314→            //    data class Foo
315→            //        @Bar1
316→            //        constructor()
317→            node
318→                .treeParent
319→                .treeParent
320→                .findChildByType(CONSTRUCTOR_KEYWORD)
321→                ?.let { constructorKeyword ->
322→                    val prevLeaf = constructorKeyword.prevLeaf()
323→                    if (prevLeaf?.elementType == WHITE_SPACE && !prevLeaf.textContains('\n')) {
324→                        emit(constructorKeyword.startOffset, "Expected newline before 'constructor' keyword", true)
325→                        if (autoCorrect) {
326→                            constructorKeyword.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expectedIndent)
327→                        }
328→                    }
329→                }
330→        }
331→    }
